message:
perf(io): unified IoRegistration::rearm() with cached waker fast path

Introduces IoRegistration::rearm(interest, waker) that replaces the
previous two-step set_interest() + update_waker() pattern used across
all I/O resource types on the poll hot path.

The key optimization is a cached_waker field on IoRegistration that
enables Waker::will_wake() comparison before cloning.  When the polling
task hasn't changed (the overwhelmingly common case), this skips:
- An atomic ref-count bump from Waker::clone()
- A mutex acquisition on the driver slab for the waker write

The new method performs reactor re-arming (required by oneshot/polling
semantics) and waker update in a single lock acquisition, reducing the
critical section from two separate lock round-trips to one.

Migrated all call sites:
- src/net/tcp/{listener,stream}.rs (including connect registration)
- src/net/udp.rs
- src/net/unix/{datagram,listener,stream}.rs
- src/process.rs

All sites now use the same match-based error handling pattern:
  Ok(true)  → registration still valid, return
  Ok(false) → slab slot gone, clear registration and re-register
  Err(NotConnected) → wake and re-register
  Err(other) → propagate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
